Defendant Roxanne Gould'S Motion For A Protective Order For Defendant, Roxanne Gould. (Part 2 Of 2 For Tentative Ruling Only)
Off Calendar Apr-18-2019 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion For A Protective Order For Defendant, Roxanne Gould
Off Calendar Apr-18-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ROXANNE GOULD's Motion For A Protective Order For Defendant, Roxanne Gould.

Defendant's motion for a protective order and Plaintiffs' request for monetary sanctions are denied. Plaintiff mailed his Form Interrogatories (Set One), Request for Production of Documents (Set One), Request for Admissions (Set One), and Special Interrogatories (Set One) to Victoria Townsend of Hartsukyer, Stratman & Williams-Abrego on November 18, 2019. Pursuant to the Notice of Change of Handling Attorney filed in this case on October 3, 2018, however, Ms. Townsend was no longer Defendant's counsel of record. Accordingly, service of the instant discovery requests was not complete until Defendant's counsel of record accepted service of them on January 23, 2019. Because Defendant's responses were timely, there is no basis for entering a protective order under Cal. Code Civ. Proc. 2030.290 and 2031.300. The Court makes no determination as to the adequacy of Defendant's discovery responses or whether any boilerplate objections warrant sanctions. Pursuant to the Court's inherent power and CCP 473, the Court sua sponte extends Plaintiff's deadline to move to compel further responses to the instant discovery requests to May 16, 2019. Prior to the initiation of any such motion, the parties must meet and confer, including by telephone, to make "a serious attempt to obtain an informal resolution of each issue." (Townsend v. Super. Ct. (1998) 61 Cal.App.4th 1431, 1435 (internal quotation marks omitted); see Cal. Civ. Proc. Code 2016.040 ("A meet and confer declaration in support of a motion shall state facts showing a reasonable and good faith attempt at an informal resolution of each issue presented by the motion."); see also Cal. Civ. Proc. Code 2023.010(i) ("Misuses of the discovery process include . . . [f]ailing to confer . . . in a reasonable and good faith attempt to resolve informally any dispute concerning discovery . . . .").)

Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312 directly to the Judge Pro Tem at mpritt@bsfllp.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Maxell Pritt, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
